message:
feat: auto-dismiss Claude Code feedback prompt (Escape)

Claude Code periodically injects an optional "How is Claude doing this
session?" feedback poll that sits in the TUI input area and blocks the
next /loop firing from submitting. The supervisor already auto-approves
sensitive-file prompts with Down+Enter; adding a parallel auto-dismiss
pattern that sends Escape for non-blocking prompts the operator doesn't
want to engage with.

- AGENT_AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES env var (newline-separated list of phrases)
- dismiss_prompts_if_present() called every poll tick alongside the
  existing approve_prompt_if_present()
- Default example.env preloaded with the feedback-poll phrase

Spotted live when the prompt caught a scanner mid-iteration; without
this, the /loop firing after the prompt appeared would stall until the
healthcheck respawned.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -185,6 +185,7 @@ All configuration lives in `/etc/supervised-agent/agent.env`. Systemd loads it v
 | `AGENT_READY_TIMEOUT_SEC` | no | `45` | Max seconds to wait for the ready marker |
 | `AGENT_POLL_SEC` | no | `10` | Supervisor's health-check interval |
 | `AGENT_AUTO_APPROVE_PHRASE` | no | `Yes, and always allow access to` | If present in the pane, supervisor auto-sends `Down Enter` (handy for Claude Code's "sensitive file" prompt). Set blank to disable. |
+| `AGENT_AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES` | no | (blank) | Newline-separated list of phrases to dismiss with `Escape` when seen. Useful for Claude Code's periodic "How is Claude doing this session?" feedback poll. |
 | `AGENT_LOG_FILE` | yes | — | Path the agent appends its heartbeat to; healthcheck watches mtime |
 | `AGENT_STALE_MAX_SEC` | no | `1800` | Seconds of log silence before healthcheck considers the agent stalled |
 | `AGENT_MAX_RESPAWNS` | no | `3` | Consecutive failed respawns before healthcheck stops auto-respawning and sends an escalation ntfy |

@@ -16,6 +16,12 @@ POLL_SEC="${AGENT_POLL_SEC:-10}"
 READY_TIMEOUT_SEC="${AGENT_READY_TIMEOUT_SEC:-45}"
 READY_MARKER="${AGENT_READY_MARKER:-bypass permissions on}"
 AUTO_APPROVE_PHRASE="${AGENT_AUTO_APPROVE_PHRASE:-}"
+# Newline-separated list of phrases that, when present in the pane, should be
+# dismissed with a single Escape keypress. Use this for non-blocking prompts
+# you don't want to respond to (e.g. Claude Code's "How is Claude doing this
+# session?" feedback poll, which otherwise sits in the input area and blocks
+# the next /loop firing).
+AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES="${AGENT_AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES:-}"
 
 log() { printf '[%s] %s\n' "$(date -Is)" "$*"; }
 
@@ -61,6 +67,22 @@ approve_prompt_if_present() {
   fi
 }
 
+dismiss_prompts_if_present() {
+  # No-op if AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES is blank.
+  [ -z "$AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES" ] && return 0
+  local pane phrase
+  pane=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p 2>/dev/null) || return 0
+  while IFS= read -r phrase; do
+    [ -z "$phrase" ] && continue
+    if echo "$pane" | grep -qF "$phrase"; then
+      log "auto-dismissing pending prompt (Escape) — matched: $phrase"
+      tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Escape
+      sleep 2
+      return 0
+    fi
+  done <<< "$AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES"
+}
+
 session_alive() { tmux has-session -t "$SESSION" 2>/dev/null; }
 
 agent_alive() {
@@ -91,6 +113,7 @@ while true; do
     start_session
   else
     approve_prompt_if_present
+    dismiss_prompts_if_present
   fi
   sleep "$POLL_SEC"
 done

@@ -58,6 +58,14 @@ AGENT_POLL_SEC=10
 # Leave blank to disable auto-approval.
 AGENT_AUTO_APPROVE_PHRASE=Yes, and always allow access to
 
+# Newline-separated list of phrases to dismiss with a single Escape keypress
+# whenever they appear. Use this for non-blocking prompts you don't want to
+# engage with (e.g. Claude Code's post-session feedback poll, which otherwise
+# sits in the input area and stops the next /loop firing from submitting).
+# Each line is matched as a fixed string (grep -F); put one phrase per line
+# inside single quotes to preserve newlines in systemd EnvironmentFile syntax.
+AGENT_AUTO_DISMISS_PHRASES='How is Claude doing this session?'
+
 # Seconds of heartbeat silence before healthcheck considers the agent stalled.
 # Default is generous: typical /loop cron cadence is 15 min, scans take 5-10
 # min, so 30 min of silence is real trouble.
